skill.name: face-swap
skill.description: Swap a face / character into video or images on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across community Wan 2-2 Animate (audio-driven character animation + identity swap), GPT Image 2 Edit (single-shot precise face swap on still images via reference composition), Nano Banana Edit (batch identity-preserving swap), Flux Kontext (single-ref high-fidelity local face edit), and Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro (transfer motion from one performance onto a target character). Picks the right model for the user's actual intent — single still vs video, full character vs face only, dialog scene vs silent motion. Triggers on "face swap", "swap face", "deepfake", "face replacement", "character swap", "head swap", "put X's face on Y", "make this video star X", "replace the actor in this video", "swap the character in the photo", "deepfake video", "ReActor alternative", or any explicit ask to substitute one identity for another.
skill.summary: Swap faces in images and videos using RunComfy's CLI with intelligent model routing
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 129 potential issues, but all are false positives. External commands (76) are legitimate Bash CLI invocations in markdown documentation examples, not Ruby execution. Network URLs (39) are RunComfy documentation endpoints. Filesystem access is standard CLI token storage. No prompt injection attempts detected. The skill includes comprehensive security documentation on consent, responsible use, and defense against reference asset injection.

risk_factor_evidence.explanation: All 76 external command detections are Bash CLI invocations (runcomfy run) in markdown documentation examples. The static analyzer incorrectly classified these as Ruby/shell backtick execution. The skill explicitly states 'No shell-injection surface' because prompts are passed as JSON via --input flag, not shell-expanded.

All hardcoded URLs are legitimate RunComfy documentation links and model API endpoints. No credential exfiltration or unauthorized data transmission detected. The skill explicitly allows only model-api.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.net/com domains.
Line 304 references ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json which is standard CLI token storage (mode 0600). This is the expected behavior for authenticated CLI tools, not malicious hidden file access.

low_findings.title: Face Swap Dual-Use Nature
low_findings.description: Face swap technology is dual-use and can be exploited for non-consensual intimate imagery or impersonation. The skill includes explicit consent guidance and refuses harmful requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly documents consent requirements and instructs agents to refuse harmful requests targeting real people without consent. This is appropriate risk acknowledgment, not a vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Reference Asset Injection Risk
low_findings.description: Face-swap pipelines can be targeted via injected content in reference images/audio/video URLs. The skill includes defensive guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documents this known attack surface and provides agent mitigation guidance. This is appropriate security documentation for the technology category.

content.user_title: Swap faces in images and videos with AI
content.value_statement: Face swapping requires choosing between multiple AI models for different use cases. This skill routes requests to the right model based on whether you need video or still images, single or batch processing, and identity or motion preservation.
actual_capabilities: Swap faces in videos using Wan 2-2 Animate (audio-driven) or Kling Motion Control Pro (motion-preserving)
Replace faces in still images using GPT Image 2 Edit (multi-ref), Nano Banana Edit (batch), or Flux Kontext (single-ref)
Route requests to the appropriate model based on input type and desired output characteristics
Preserve identity consistency across multiple frames in batch processing
Transfer motion from a source video onto a target character
Generate face swaps from textual descriptions when no reference image is available
limitations: Cannot process faces without consent or rights to both source and target media
Output platform may require disclosure labels for synthetic media
Batch processing limited to 1-20 images per call depending on the model route
Single-ref models cannot use multiple reference images for precision work

use_cases.title: Product marketing team creating consistent brand content
use_cases.description: Swap a brand spokesperson into multiple product scenes for A/B testing variants. Use batch processing to maintain identity across a gallery of shots.

use_cases.title: Video producer dubbing dialogue with a new actor
use_cases.description: Replace an actor's face in existing footage with a new performer who will re-record the dialogue. Audio drives the animation for lip-sync accuracy.

use_cases.title: Content creator generating character variations
use_cases.description: Test how an animated character looks in live-action scenes by transferring motion onto stylized artwork. Ideal for VFX previsualization.

prompt_templates.title: Swap a face in a video
prompt_templates.prompt: Swap [PERSON_A]'s face with [PERSON_B]'s face in this video: [VIDEO_URL]. Use [PERSON_B]'s portrait as the reference: [PORTRAIT_URL]. Preserve the original audio track.
prompt_templates.scenario: Video with audio track that needs identity replacement

prompt_templates.title: Batch face swap for multiple scenes
prompt_templates.prompt: Swap the face in each of these product photos with [MODEL_NAME]. Use this portrait as the identity reference: [PORTRAIT_URL]. Keep pose, lighting, and background unchanged. Photos: [LIST_URLS]
prompt_templates.scenario: Multiple images requiring consistent identity across the batch

prompt_templates.title: Transfer motion onto a character
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the motion from [SOURCE_VIDEO_URL] and apply it to [CHARACTER_IMAGE_URL]. The character should perform the exact same movements as the source video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Motion transfer from reference performance to target character

prompt_templates.title: Describe a face swap without a reference image
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image: [IMAGE_URL]. Keep everything exactly the same - pose, clothing, hair, lighting, background. Change only the face to match this description: [PROSE_DESCRIPTION]
prompt_templates.scenario: Single image edit with prose-based identity specification

output_examples.input: Swap the actor's face in this video with the director's face. Video: [VIDEO_URL]. Director portrait: [PORTRAIT_URL].
output_examples.output: Generated a video with the director's face animated to match the original actor's movements and lip-sync. The background and costume remain unchanged from the source video.

output_examples.input: Replace the face in these three product shots with our spokesperson. Identity: [PORTRAIT_URL]. Scenes: [SCENE_1], [SCENE_2], [SCENE_3].
output_examples.output: Produced three product images with the spokesperson's face consistently applied. Lighting and camera angles match the original product shots.

best_practices: Use clean, well-lit portraits as identity references for best results
Match lighting conditions between the reference and target scenes
Preserve the original audio track when swapping faces in video to maintain lip-sync quality
anti_patterns: Do not swap faces of real people without their explicit consent
Avoid using low-quality reference images that may degrade swap accuracy
Do not combine multiple identity references in a single-pass prompt when precise results are needed

faq.question: Which model should I use for face swapping?
faq.answer: Use Wan 2-2 Animate for video with audio, Kling Motion Control Pro for motion preservation, GPT Image 2 Edit for single still images with multiple references, Nano Banana Edit for batch processing, and Flux Kontext for single-image edits from a prose description.

faq.question: Can I swap a face without a reference image of the target person?
faq.answer: Yes. Flux Kontext allows you to describe the desired face in prose rather than providing a reference image. However, results are less predictable than using an actual portrait.

faq.question: How many images can I process in batch?
faq.answer: Nano Banana Edit supports 1-20 images per call. The first image should be the identity reference. Other routes are single-image only.

faq.question: What consent do I need before face swapping?
faq.answer: You need rights to both the target face (the identity being inserted) and the source media (the video or image being modified). Many platforms require disclosure labels for synthetic media.

faq.question: Why does my face swap look unnatural?
faq.answer: Ensure the reference portrait has similar lighting and angle to the target scene. Explicitly prompt to preserve pose, clothing, and background. Match skin tone explicitly in your prompt.

faq.question: Can I preserve the original motion in a video while swapping the face?
faq.answer: Yes. Use Kling Motion Control Pro to transfer the exact motion from a source video onto a target character image. This is different from a pure face swap which regenerates animation from audio.
